title: Kingdom of Cron
content:

[[27,000 BBY/Legends|27,000 BBY]] and c. [[25,150 BBY]] |fragmented= |reorganized=*c. [[25,130 BBY]], as [[Xer VIII|Xer's]] Empire *[[25,127 BBY]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' as part of [[Xim's empire]] *Between [[25,096 BBY]] and c. [[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]], as an independent kingdom *c. [[23,900 BBY]], as part of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] *Between [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] and [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]],''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' as the Cronese Mandate |dissolved= |restored= |affiliation=*Kingdom of Cron *Xer's Empire *Xim's empire *Independent *Galactic Republic *[[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' }} The '''Kingdom of Cron''' was a territory carved out in the [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]]. During the rule of [[Xer VIII]], the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] father of [[Xim/Legends|Xim the Despot]], it was also known as '''Xer's Empire'''. The Kingdom of Cron was governed from the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Chandaar]]. ==Description== The Kingdom of Cron was the government of the region of the [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]] later known as the [[Cronese Mandate/Legends|Cronese Mandate]]. It was ruled by the [[King of Cron]] from the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Chandaar]]. Its principal worlds were along the [[Greater Cronese Arc]]. ==History== ===Early history=== [[File:Kingdom_of_Barseg.jpg|thumb|left|210px|The Kingdom of Cron, c. [[25,150 BBY]]]] Around [[25,150 BBY]], the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Xer VIII]] began rapid [[Campaign on the Ihala Route|expansion]] from his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] [[Argai]], creating the [[Kingdom of Argai]] within the Kingdom of Cron. King of Cron [[Ferece]] summoned Xer to Chandaar, the capital of the Kingdom of Cron. Ferece tried to persuade Xer give an oath of fealty, in return for a number of [[Noble/Legends|noble]] titles, and Xer promised to return in a [[Standard month/Legends|month]] with a suitable tribute. However, Xer instead led an [[Battle of Chandaar|attack]] on Chandaar, causing Ferece and his courtiers to flee. In the absence of Ferece, Xer installed himself as the new King of Cron. ===Xer's Empire=== [[File:Xers_Empire.jpg|thumb|right|210px|The Kingdom of Cron, c. [[25,130 BBY]]]] As the new King of Cron, Xer VIII launched several [[Xer's Campaigns|campaigns]] to expand his territory. He conquered the [[Three Allied Kingdoms]] and the [[Back Spiral]]. However he didn't manage to [[Campaign against the Livien League|conquer]] the [[Livien League]], the only part of the Tion Cluster out of his control. Around [[25,130 BBY]], near the end of Xer's reign, the Kingdom of Cron was divided in three provinces, very similar to the three future [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Sector/Legends|sectors]], which one of them was the original Kingdom of Cron. ===Xim's empire=== [[File:Xims_Empire_small.jpg|thumb|left|210px|The Kingdom of Cron, c. [[25,100 BBY]]]] {{Main|Xim's empire}} Later, his son [[Xim/Legends|Xim the Despot]] used the Kingdom of Cron as the base for an even more expansive [[Xim's empire|empire]], firstly by [[Battle of Jhantoria|conquering]] the Livien League and his rule soon stretched from the [[Radama Void/Legends|Radama Void]] to [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]]. During that [[Xim's Expansionist Period|time]], Chandaar stayed the capital of the kingdom but Argai became the capital of the empire. ===Later history=== [[File:XimsEmpireSuccessorStates-XimWeek.jpg|thumb|right|210px|The Kingdom of Cron, c. [[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]]]] Following the defeat of Xim at the [[Third Battle of Vontor]] in [[25,096 BBY]], his empire quickly collapsed and new states were quickly formed out of the former territory of the empire. The Kingdom of Cron became independent again but its territory was considerably reduced, similar to the original kingdom. Around [[24,000 BBY]], the Tion Cluster and the Kingdom of Cron joined the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. In the Republic's final [[Standard year/Legends|years]], the Tion Cluster joined the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. At this time, the Tion Cluster was shared between the Kingdom of Cron and a larger [[Tion Hegemony/Legends|Tion Hegemony]]. ===Modern history=== [[File:Cronese_Mandate.jpg|thumb|left|210px|The Cronese Mandate]] {{Main|Cronese Mandate/Legends}} Following the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the Galactic Empire divided the Tion Cluster in three sectors. The Kingdom of Cron was reorganized as the Cronese Mandate, a sector almost similar to the historical kingdom, and containing the fourteen heavily populated worlds of the former Kingdom of Cron.''[[Mission to Lianna]]'' ==Astrography== ===The Kingdom of Cron during the reign of Ferece=== *[[Algor]] *[[Argai]] *[[Barancar]] *[[Chandaar]] {{C|capital of the Kingdom of Cron}} *[[Corlass]] *Soruus *Tion ==Known Kings of Cron== {{Main|King of Cron}} [[File:Sullustan_Despotica.jpg|thumb|right|235px|A [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] production of ''[[The Despotica]]'' depicting [[Oziaf]] and Xim presenting Ferece's head to Xer VIII]] {{Quote|My own hands bear to you, father, the head of your most bitter enemy, Ferece, the Cowardly King, and on his blood-drenched ringlets that which you have sought since birth: the crown of Cron.''"
"''Cron? Cron is dead?''"
"''Cron is ours.|Xim and Xer VIII, in the play ''[[The Pirate Prince]]''|{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/ximweek03/index.html|text=Xim Week: ''The Despotica'' (Part II: The Pirate Prince)|int=Xim Week: The Despotica}}}} *[[Ferece]] *[[Xer VIII]] *[[Xim/Legends|Xim]] ==Behind the scenes== ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' erroneously refers to Xer's Empire as forming around [[25,120 BBY]], a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] after Xim had already assumed the throne. ==Appearances== *''[[Beyond the Rim]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Mission to Lianna]]'' {{1stm}} ==Sources== *''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *{{CSWECite|multiple}} *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-the-history-of-xim-and-the-tion-cluster|text=Essential Atlas Extra: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster|int=Xim Week: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster}} *{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/ximweek03/index.html|text=Xim Week: ''The Despotica'' (Part II: The Pirate Prince)|int=Xim Week: The Despotica}} *{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/ximweek05/index.html|text=Xim Week: ''The Despotica'' (Part IV: ''Evocar'')|int=Xim Week: The Despotica}} {{ID|Cron}} *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/writtenword/index.html?page=1|text=The Written Word|int=The Written Word}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' *{{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-3-xim-the-despot|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 3 - "Xim the Despot"}} {{ID|Cron}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Reino de Cron }} [[Category:Confederacy of Independent Systems-aligned governments]] [[Category:Galactic Empire-aligned governments]] [[Category:Galactic Republic-aligned governments]] [[Category:Tion Cluster historic states]] [[Category:Xim's empire]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Kingdom of Cron]] }}
